title: Early growth observation for 3 Sisters Plus planting
summary: Initial observation of uneven corn emergence, companion plant presence, good moisture retention, and moderate slug pressure in the Spring 2026 3 Sisters Plus trial.
description: Initial field observation of germination, soil moisture behavior, companion emergence, and early pest pressure in the Spring 2026 3 Sisters Plus planting at the Veneta stewardship site. The hugelkultur bed appears to be retaining moisture well despite short dry intervals between rains.

observed_entities.label: Spring 2026 3 Sisters Plus planting
observed_entities.notes: Primary planting context for this observation.

observed_entities.label: Brassica spp.
observed_entities.notes: Volunteer or companion seedlings present at margins.

observed_entities.label: Slugs
observed_entities.notes: Visible feeding pressure on some emerging seedlings.

environmental_context.weather_notes: Intermittent rain, mild temperatures, and cool nights typical of spring in the Long Tom watershed.
environmental_context.soil_moisture_notes: Moderate moisture with good retention in the hugelkultur bed.
recent_events: recent bed preparation with mulch and organic matter

measurements.notes: Rough count from visible emerged corn plants in the observed section.

qualitative_signals.description: Surface mulch and underlying bed structure appear to be moderating moisture loss and improving bed texture.
Slug activity visible on several emerging seedlings.
A few native bees observed nearby, though flowering is still limited.

interpretations.statement: The hugelkultur structure may be improving moisture retention during early spring establishment.
interpretations.basis: Surface conditions remain moist despite short dry intervals between rainfall events.

interpretations.statement: Uneven corn emergence may reflect variation in planting depth, seed vigor, or microdifferences in moisture.
interpretations.basis: Seedlings are present but patchy across the bed.

interpretations.statement: Volunteer or companion mustard may be adding an unplanned crucifer element to the polyculture.
interpretations.basis: Mustard seedlings are present around the bed margins.

uncertainties: Bean and squash emergence may still occur later and should not yet be interpreted as failure.
Fungal activity is suspected beneath the woody layers but has not been directly measured.
Observed slug pressure may increase or decrease depending on the next weather interval.

follow_up_actions: Continue monitoring bean and squash emergence.
Observe slug pressure closely over the next week.
Record bed moisture after the next dry interval.
Compare emergence patterns across nearby hugelkultur beds.

provenance.source_ref: field notebook entry for Veneta 3 Sisters observation

agent_assistance.notes: Assisted with aligning the example record to the observation schema.
